design_philosophy.core_loop: Swim through underwater levels, shoot bubbles to trap enemies, dig for treasure, collect power-ups to strengthen abilities, progress toward Ursula's Castle
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of bubble power/range vs. enemy threats; exploration-based treasure hunting for upgrades
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Ariel navigating the underwater world, using her bubble-shooting ability as primary combat mechanic, collecting treasures and power-ups to grow stronger
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Character-driven action game targeting female audiences with a Disney license property; emphasizes exploration and treasure collection alongside combat rather than pure platforming challenge

difficulty.lives_system: Health icons (hearts) scattered throughout levels; collecting heart icons restores health
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with six stages total; stages begin with Ariel descending from top of screen to starting position
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-up collection directly affects combat viability; treasure chest rewards provide bubble power and range upgrades that impact player capability

progression.upgrade_system: Bubble power and range upgrades obtained from treasure chests; health restoration and extra lives available via scattered icons
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six sequential stages: The Coral Sea → Sunken Ship → Sea of Ice → Undersea Volcano → Ursula's Castle (two-part finale)
replayability_hooks: Treasure hunting mechanic rewards exploration and digging through sand for hidden collectibles
Female-focused marketing suggests designed for sustained engagement among primary audience

world_building_techniques: Stage-based progression follows film plot structure
Thematic level design reflecting film locations (Coral Sea, Sunken Ship, Sea of Ice, Volcano)
Ursula positioned as final boss/climactic destination matching film narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Ursula serves as penultimate threat across two consecutive battles at her castle; positioning suggests escalating challenge leading to climax

faction_indicators: Strong female protagonist (Ariel) as player character
Disney license positioning game within broader Renaissance era media phenomenon
danger_signals: Underwater setting with hostile creatures and environmental hazards
Sea witch antagonist (Ursula) as narrative threat
environmental_storytelling: Sunken ship location suggests nautical history and danger
Sea of Ice implies environmental hazards beyond enemy combat
Undersea Volcano provides climactic location building
Ursula's Castle represents ultimate destination and threat convergence

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - The Little Mermaid (NES)
